editorialContract.philosophy: Stars are the review. Commentary is an in-character reaction to the exhibit, not a software review.

continuityNotes: The Mirror Menagerie Keeper watches rain, seams, tilt, rosettes, reduced motion, and clean teardown through the glass; the Reflection answers with one bloom on its own side.
The Patron keeps the private watchlist's search, type filters, sort, pagination, stats, and entry updates from abandonment; Yuki leaves the same title a quiet place to wait.

The Overqualified Candidate already knows autosave, immediate save, metadata patches, ratings, publication, and external links; Weirdlandia turns the same entry detail into a door with several warm handles.

revisions.editedComment: Come closer, but not too close to the glass—hush, love. Rain follows the lead seams; your movement tilts the color, and a touch blooms one rosette through the pane. Reduced motion quiets the performance. The frame and listeners leave when the window closes. I still cannot tell which side is watching.
You touched the glass. The color bloomed on my side first.

The odds say the private shelf will outlive your attention; that is why I’m here. Admins may search, filter by type, sort, page through more entries, inspect stats, and update one title through its detail panel. Nothing is abandoned merely because it is unfinished.
A title may wait here. Search softly. Star it. Return when the snow is quieter.

You’re wondering whether the private draft saves before publication. It does—after the pause, or immediately when asked. Star, consumed date, year, rating, review, published state, and external links patch the same entry. I already knew which control you would touch.
The entry is a door with several handles: star, date, rating, draft, publish, link. Choose carefully. One handle is already warm.
